title: Device for target monitoring and method thereof
abstract: The present invention provides a system for indicating hitting a target, comprising: a. at least one target; and b. at least one sensor configured to generate at least one signal correlated with at least one parameter of motion of said target wherein said parameter of motion of said target results from an impact thereon indicating a hitting of said target.
claims:
Claims A system for indicating hitting a target, comprising: a. at least one target; and b. at least one sensor configured to generate at least one signal correlated with at least one parameter of motion of said target wherein said parameter of motion of said target results from an impact thereon indicating a hitting of said target. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, additionally comprising at least one processor in communication with said at least one sensor, said processor configured to determine, from said at least one signal, occurrence of at least one said hit on said target. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, wherein said target is configured to move upon said impact thereon so that a second hit is on an undamaged portion of said target. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, additionally comprising at least one sensor plate connectable to said target by a plurality of first lines, said target supportable from above by said sensor plate. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 4, wherein said at least one sensor is mountable to said sensor plate. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 4, wherein at least one of said plurality of at least one first lines comprises non-extensible line. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, additionally comprising a support connectable to said sensor plate by at least one second line, said sensor plate supportable from above by said support. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 7, additionally comprising at least one spring, said spring connectable between said support and said at least one second line. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 7, wherein motion of said target is substantially unaffected by said support. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 7, wherein a member of a group consisting of said plurality of first lines, said at least on second line and any combination thereof is selected from a group consisting of a wire, a string, a cable, a 

 chain and any combination thereof. 11. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 7, wherein said support is selected from a group consisting of a ground-based support and an unmanned vehicle. 12. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 11, wherein said unmanned vehicle is selected from the group consisting of an aerial vehicle, a ground vehicle, or a marine vehicles and any combination thereof. 13. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, wherein said at least one sensor is selected from a group consisting of: a motion sensor, an acceleration sensor, a velocity sensor, an angular motion sensor, an angular velocity sensor, an angular acceleration sensor, a MEMS sensor, a gyroscope, a digital compass and any combination thereof. 14. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, wherein said target comprises a hollow cylinder. 15. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, wherein said target is substantially rigid. 16. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, wherein said target comprises a mesh. 17. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 16, wherein said mesh is selected from a group consisting of a woven mesh and a perforated mesh. 18. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 17, wherein a shape of a perforation in said perforated mesh is selected from a group consisting of: oval, elliptical, polygonal, and any combination thereof. 19. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 18, wherein said polygonal shape perforation can have a number of sides between 3 and about 20. 20. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 19, wherein said sides can be straight or curved. 21. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 19, wherein at least two of said sides have the same length or different lengths. 22. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 19, wherein at least two of said perforations are the same size or different sizes. 23. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 19, wherein at least three of said perforations can be aligned in a pattern selected from: aligned vertically, aligned horizontally, with horizontal rows offset from each other, with vertical rows offset from each other, in a hexagonal array, and any combination thereof. 24. The system for indicating hitting a target of claim 1, additionally comprising a display 

 configured to display said location of said at least one impact on said target. 25. A method for indicating hitting a target, comprising steps of: a. providing a system for indicating hitting a target, comprising: i. at least one target; and ii. at least one sensor configured to generate at least one signal correlated with at least one parameter of motion of said target; b. upon a hit on said target, said sensor indicating said hit by generating said at least one signal correlated with at least one parameter of motion of said target; wherein said parameter of motion of said target results from an impact thereon indicating a hitting of said target. 26. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of providing at least one processor in communication with said at least one sensor, said processor configured to determine, from said signal, occurrence of at least one said hit on said target. 27. The method of claim 26, additionally comprising a step of communicating at least one signal from said at least one sensor to said at least one processor. 28. The method of claim 27, additionally comprising a step of determining said location of at least one impact on said target by analysis of said signal by said processor 29. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of said impact moving said target so that a second hit is on an undamaged portion of said target. 30. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of providing at least one sensor plate connectable to said target by a plurality of first lines, said target supportable from above by said sensor plate. 31. The method of claim 30, additionally comprising a step of attaching said sensor plate to said target via said plurality of first lines. 32. The method of claim 30, additionally comprising a step of mounting said at least one sensor to said sensor plate. 33. The method of claim 30, additionally comprising a step of providing at least one of said plurality of at least one first lines as a non-extensible line. 34. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of providing a support connectable to said sensor plate by at least one second line, said sensor plate supportable from above by said support. 35. The method of claim 34, additionally comprising a step of attaching said sensor plate to said support via said at least one second line. 36. The method of claim 35, additionally comprising a step of connecting at least one spring between said support and said at least one second line. 37. The method of claim 34, wherein motion of said target is substantially unaffected by said support. 38. The method of claim 34, additionally comprising a step of selecting a member of a group consisting of said plurality of first lines, said at least on second line and any combination thereof from a group consisting of a wire, a string, a cable, a chain and any combination thereof. 39. The method of claim 34, additionally comprising a step of selecting said support from a group consisting of a ground-based support and an unmanned vehicle. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein said unmanned vehicle is selected from the group consisting of an aerial vehicle, a ground vehicle, or a marine vehicles and any combination thereof. 41. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of selecting said at least one sensor from a group consisting of: a motion sensor, an acceleration sensor, a velocity sensor, an angular motion sensor, an angular velocity sensor, an angular acceleration sensor, a MEMS sensor, a gyroscope, a digital compass and any combination thereof. 42. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of providing said target comprising a hollow cylinder. 43. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of selecting said target to be substantially rigid. 44. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising a step of providing said target comprising a mesh. 45. The method of claim 44, additionally comprising a step of selecting said mesh from a group consisting of a woven mesh and a perforated mesh. 46. The method of claim 45, additionally comprising a step of selecting a shape of a perforation in said perforated mesh from a group consisting of: oval, elliptical, polygonal, and any combination thereof. 47. The method of claim 46, additionally comprising a step of selecting a number of sides for said polygonal shape perforation to be in a range from 3 and about 20. 48. The method of claim 47, additionally comprising a step of selecting said sides to be either straight or curved. 49. The method of claim 47, additionally comprising a step of selecting, for at least two of said sides, for said at least two sides to have the same length or different lengths. 50. The method of claim 47, additionally comprising a step of selecting, for at least two of said perforations, at least two of said perforations to be the same size or different sizes. 51. The method of claim 47, additionally comprising a step of selecting, for at least three of said perforations, at least three of said perforations to be aligned in a pattern selected from: aligned vertically, aligned horizontally, with horizontal rows offset from each other, with vertical rows offset from each other, in a hexagonal array, and any combination thereof. 52. The method of claim 25, additionally comprising steps of providing a display and of displaying said location of said at least one impact on said target.
